versions.notes: Early model — logistic regression on synthetic lines only, no lineValue factor, no date-windowing

Added lineValue factor (20%), exponential weighting in hitRate, recency decay (dataFreshness), date-windowed factors (60-day lineValue, 90-day hitRate/trend), fixed UNDER reason text. HIGH threshold 73.
issues: Only 22 HIGH picks — threshold 73 too high without live matchup/injury/spread factors
HIGH hit rate 36.4% — small sample dominated by noisy picks

versions.notes: Weight rebalancing after backtest: lineValue 20%→24% (most validated), matchupEdge 22%→18% (less validated), trend 12%→13%. HIGH threshold lowered 73→70 (70-79 bucket showed 57.1% HR). Stat-specific HIGH thresholds: assists/pra require ≥78 (overconfident at 70 in backtest).
issues: Backtest uses log-based factors only (38% of live weight defaults to neutral 0.50)
assists/pra HIGH thresholds raised to 78 — need more data to validate
steals HIGH sample (18 props) still small — watch for regression
next_improvements: Player consistency modifier — CV-based adjustment per player/stat
Star player factor — top-usage players get lower variance discount
Minutes stability — variable-minutes players should score lower
Per-player weight profiles based on player archetype

versions.notes: Attempted consistency (CV-based) and minutes stability modifiers as score adjustments. Finding: variance is already priced into hitRate/lineValue — additive penalties caused false negatives (good picks pushed below threshold). Reverted adjustments to informational reason-text only. Added three_pointers to elevated HIGH threshold (76) since it's inherently volatile. Modifiers kept as user-facing warnings only.
key_learnings: Stat-specific HIGH thresholds outperform blanket variance modifiers
hitRate + lineValue already price in normal variance — explicit CV penalty = double-counting
three_pointers elevated to 76: inherently high CV, showed 36.4% HR when included at 70
assists/pra at 78: script-dependent / combo stat, hard for model to call HIGH reliably
Consistency/minutes warnings useful for UX but not score-affecting
next_improvements: Star player factor — high-usage/star players might deserve different weight profiles
Per-player line bias — track if a player consistently beats/misses their L10 avg
Opponent-specific adjustments — some teams leak specific stats regardless of rank
Parlay correlation — when bundling HIGH picks, check stat correlation

versions.notes: Removed dead/harmful factors: consistency (weight 0.00, no predictive power), statConsistencyAdj + minutesStabilityAdj (additive penalties caused false negatives — variance already priced by hitRate/lineValue), W_STAR/W_ROTATION weight profiles (caused regression — matchupEdge neutral in log-only backtest). Added star bonus: +3 pts for players averaging ≥36 min when both lineValue ≥0.58 AND hitRate ≥0.55. Additive-only, never penalizes. Stars are matchup-proof so log-based signals are more reliable for them.

star_bonus.condition: playerTier === 'star' (≥36 min avg) AND fLineValue ≥ 0.58 AND f7 (hitRate) ≥ 0.55
star_bonus.rationale: Stars are matchup-proof — log signals more reliable; additive-only

versions.tier_results: not recorded (superseded by v5.5 optimizer run)
next_improvements: Run weight optimizer to find data-driven weight distribution
Per-player line bias — track actual/line ratio per player to detect book bias
Opponent-specific leaks — some teams give up specific stats vs their rank

versions.notes: Weight optimizer: 5,000 Dirichlet random samples × 34,163 real props. Key finding: lineValue was overweighted at 24% — largely redundant with last20HitRate (both measure avg vs line). Shifting weight from lineValue → hitRate/trend/restDays improved HIGH hit rate from 58.0% to 63.8%, with fewer but more accurate HIGH picks. Star bonus gate relaxed from fLineValue ≥ 0.58 to f7 (hitRate) ≥ 0.60 to match new weight emphasis.

star_bonus.rationale: Gate switched from lineValue to hitRate to match new weight emphasis

key_learnings: lineValue redundant with hitRate — both measure avg vs line; hitRate is more robust (handles variance implicitly)
restDays was significantly underweighted — back-to-back effect is meaningful
trend (L5 vs L20) consistently appears in top optimizer results — validated signal
Optimizer reduces HIGH count (69 vs 88) but raises hit rate enough to double ROI
Neutral backtest factors (matchupEdge, pace, injury) optimized at current weights — will become more meaningful with live data
next_improvements: Per-player line bias — track each player's actual/line ratio to detect systematic book mispricing
Opponent-specific leaks — some teams give up specific stats regardless of overall rank
Run optimizer on combined (real + synthetic) dataset for larger sample validation
Consider raising MIN_HIGH threshold further (80+) to filter to ultra-high-confidence only

versions.notes: Second optimizer run (5,000 Dirichlet samples, MIN_HIGH=80 props floor for volume+quality balance). Key shifts: matchupEdge 0.18→0.04 (neutral in backtest — confirmed near-zero signal), last20HitRate 0.25→0.30 (dominant factor when volume floor applied), blowout 0.04→0.11 (consistently elevated across all optimizer runs), trend 0.15→0.09 (less standalone predictive than expected). Also introduced 4-tier labeling system (LOCK/PLAY/LEAN/FADE) replacing HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW.
key_learnings: matchupEdge confirmed near-zero signal in log-only backtest (0.18→0.04 has no regression cost)
blowout emerged as meaningful factor consistently across optimizer runs — was severely underweighted at 0.04
last20HitRate is the dominant predictive factor — 30% weight justified
4-tier system (LOCK/PLAY/LEAN/FADE) gives better granularity — LOCK is the premium bet tier
Score ceiling ~73 with current weights — thresholds must be calibrated to actual distribution, not arbitrary 80+
next_improvements: Per-player line bias — track each player's actual/line ratio to detect systematic book mispricing
Opponent-specific leaks — some teams give up specific stats regardless of overall rank
Increase LOCK volume: star bonus fires rarely — consider broader star bonus criteria

versions.notes: Added player_line_bias and opponent_stat_leaks as additive score adjustments. player_line_bias: (hit_rate - 0.50) × min(n/20,1) × 10, capped ±5pts, min 6 samples (777 entries). opponent_stat_leaks: (over_hit_rate - 0.50) × min(n/40,1) × 8, capped ±4pts, min 10 samples (183 entries). Both adjustments feed into backtest scoring via ScoringContext.
key_learnings: Player bias adjustment meaningfully improves PLAY tier (+3.8pp hit rate)
Opponent leaks promote some borderline props into LOCK — dilutes LOCK hit rate slightly
Combined LOCK+PLAY tells the real story: +3.3pp improvement and +33 more picks
Bias/leak are retrospective signals in backtest (same data used to compute and test) — live performance may differ
Key leaks: SAC leaky across all stats, NY/LAL leak assists, CHI/DAL leak steals, MIN leaks 3PM
next_improvements: Re-run weight optimizer with bias+leak in scoring to find new optimal weights
Increase LOCK volume: star bonus rarely fires — broaden criteria or raise star bonus value
Consider time-decaying leak/bias data (recent games weighted more than old)
